patch.value:
## Context
Extension hooks (registerHook) fire only in-process during a mutation. Cross-process, the only way another agent learns about workspace changes is polling (pm context / pm list), which at thousands of concurrent agents is both token-expensive and racy. The pm-slack package (lifecycle notifications) exists as prior art for the *consumer* side but has no first-class producer primitive to build on.

## Proposal
A read-only mutation event stream derived from the append-only history streams (no new write path, no daemon — consistent with ADR pm-i7i4):
1. CLI: `pm events --follow [--since <cursor>] [--type ...] [--author ...] [--item ...]` emitting one JSON object per line (NDJSON, per the pm-646c convention) so it composes with grep/jq/xargs (pm-m9xv story, pm-psy1 scripting contract).
2. SDK: a subscription/iterator primitive (async iterable over history events with a durable cursor) — the layer packages like pm-slack, pm-web, and future coordination tooling rebuild on.
3. Cursor semantics: stable resumable position (per-stream offsets or global logical clock) so an agent can disconnect and catch up without replaying everything; must stay O(new events) at 100k-1M items, which couples this to the pm-9rxu read-index work.

## Non-goals
- No long-running server/daemon requirement: --follow is an optional convenience over poll-with-cursor; single-shot `pm events --since <cursor>` must work for stateless agents.
- Not a message bus: events are facts about mutations already committed to history, never commands.

## Relationships
- Parent pm-dj98 (multi-agent safety): reduces polling contention and enables claim/handoff coordination patterns.
- pm-646c (NDJSON), pm-psy1 (scripting contract), pm-9rxu/pm-801d (index makes tailing cheap), pm-usfg (SDK primitive first, CLI shim second), pm-slack package (first consumer to migrate).
- Horizon: mid-to-long — after NDJSON output mode and the persistent read index land.

## Duplicate check (2026-07-07 audit #16)
Searched "watch mode event subscription", "watch follow tail stream", "pm watch subscribe mutations", "notification agent coordination" — no existing item covers a cross-process event/subscription primitive; nearest matches were in-process hook work and the pm-slack package (consumer only).
Mutation event stream primitive: pm events --follow (NDJSON with durable cursor) + SDK subscription API for cross-process agent coordination

Read-only, cursor-resumable event feed derived from the append-only history streams so thousands of agents can react to workspace changes without polling: NDJSON CLI surface composable with grep/jq, SDK async-iterator primitive underneath, no daemon required (single-shot --since works for stateless agents). First consumer to migrate: the pm-slack package.

pm events emits one JSON object per line with a stable resumable cursor; filterable by type/author/item; single-shot --since and --follow modes both work; SDK subscription primitive documented and used by the CLI shim; catch-up cost is O(new events) at scale-index workspaces; pm-slack migration path noted

author: maintainer-agent
message: Event feed builds on NDJSON convention and persistent read index
